sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good, the gray bar (id=1) is still at [9,27]-[11,27] with 3 pixels. The yellow bar is still intact at rows 32-35, cols 9-11.
So the current green box state is:
Row 9: G N N (cols 27, 28, 29)
Row 10: G r N (cols 27, 28, 29)
Row 11: G N N (cols 27, 28, 29)
The green box (N) has 5 pixels: (9,28), (9,29), (10,29), (11,28), (11,29). It's a C-shape opening to the left (the left column has the red dot at [10,28] instead of green).
The gray bar (G) is at col 27, rows 9-11.
Now let me think about what happened when I clicked at [10, 29]:
- The green box was originally a 3x5 rectangle (cols 28-32, rows 9-11) with 14 pixels (one empty for the red dot at [10,31]).
- After clicking at [10, 29], the box shrank to 5 pixels and the red dot moved to [10, 28].
- The cells that were consumed: the right side of the box (cols 30-32) and some interior cells.
Wait, let me think about this more carefully. The original box was:
Row 9: N N N N N (cols 28-32)
Row 10: N N N N r (cols 28-32, with r at col 31)
Row 11: N N N N N (cols 28-32)
That's 14 N pixels + 1 r pixel = 15 cells total.
After clicking at [10, 29]:
Row 9: N N (cols 28-29)
Row 10: r N (cols 28-29)
Row 11: N N (cols 28-29)
That's 5 N pixels + 1 r pixel = 6 cells total.
So the box went from 14 N pixels to 5 N pixels, losing 9 pixels. The red dot moved from [10, 31] to [10, 28].
The click was at [10, 29], which was an N pixel. After the click, [10, 29] is still N. So the click didn't erase the clicked cell.
Hmm, what if the mechanic is: clicking on a cell inside the box causes the box to shrink from the OPPOSITE side? I clicked at [10, 29] (left side of the interior), and the box shrank from the RIGHT side (cols 30-32 were removed).
If that's the case, clicking on the right side of the box would make it shrink from the left side.
Let me test this by clicking on [10, 29] again (which is now on the right edge of the remaining box). If the mechanic is "shrink from opposite side", clicking on the right edge should shrink from the left, removing col 28.
Actually wait, the box is now only 2 columns wide (cols 28-29). If I click on [10, 29] (right edge), the opposite side is the left (col 28). Shrinking from the left would remove col 28, leaving only col 29.
But [10, 28] currently has the red dot, not a green pixel. So shrinking from the left would remove the red dot's position.
Let me try clicking on [9, 29] (top-right corner of the remaining box) to see what happens.
Actually, I realize I should think about this more systematically. The initial box was 3x5 (rows 9-11, cols 28-32). After clicking at [10, 29], it became 3x2 (rows 9-11, cols 28-29). The click column was 29, and the box shrank from the right, keeping cols 28-29.
So the rule might be: clicking on a cell causes the box to shrink to the LEFT of the click column (inclusive). The click was at col 29, and the box now spans cols 28-29 (everything up to and including col 29).
Wait, but the original box spanned cols 28-32. After clicking at col 29, it spans cols 28-29. So the box was truncated at the click column from the right.
Similarly, maybe clicking on a cell causes the box to shrink to the RIGHT of the click column. If I click at col 30, the box would span cols 30-32. But I clicked at [10, 29], not [10, 30].
Hmm, let me think again. The click was at [10, 29]. The box originally spanned cols 28-32. After the click, it spans cols 28-29. The click column is 29, and the box was truncated to end at column 29.
So the rule is: clicking on a cell at column C causes the box to be truncated to columns [left_edge, C]. The box keeps everything from the left edge up to and including the click column.
If this is correct, clicking at [10, 28] (the leftmost column) would truncate the box to just column 28, leaving only 3 pixels (rows 9-11 at col 28). But [10, 28] has the red dot, not a green pixel. Can I click on the red dot?
Let me try clicking on [10, 28] (the red dot position).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me test the hypothesis: clicking at column C truncates the box to [left_edge, C]
# Current box spans cols 28-29. Let me click at [10, 28] (the red dot) to see if it truncates to just col 28.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 28}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
